frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions arise when accumulated evidence reaches a terminating threshold

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: noisy accumulation of momentary evidence to a bound governs choice and timing

frameworks.name: threshold-crossing account of conscious awareness
frameworks.one_line: subjective 'aha' moment indexes the moment evidence first hits the bound

frameworks.name: post hoc inference account
frameworks.one_line: alternative that subjective decision reports are reconstructed rather than registered

core_question: Do subjective reports of when one decided correspond to an evidence accumulation reaching a termination threshold?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions reflect noisy accumulation of evidence
a terminating bound exists and its crossing is psychologically registrable
subjective decision time can be measured by a clock-setting report
manipulations: decoupling of overt response time from subjective decision time
key_findings: subjective decision times were faster on easier (stronger motion) trials
subjective decision time distributions were well fit by a bounded drift-diffusion model
parameters fit to subjective decision times alone predicted choice accuracy in four of five participants
interpretations: subjective reports of deciding index the terminating step of evidence accumulation
conscious awareness of having decided arises when accumulated evidence reaches a threshold
limitations_noted: predictive match held for four of five participants
